_README: The secret / ground-truth READ DENYLIST for the defender runtime gate.
read_deny = paths the agent may never read, applied INSIDE every allowed path on BOTH
`substrings` match the FILENAME; `dirs` match any path COMPONENT. Kept as a
declarative last line (defense-in-depth) even though OS isolation is the
What used to live here and no longer does (#575): the per-agent capability bits
(adapters / adapter_sql_pipe / raw_reads). They were a second, declarative model of a
permission the gate enforced somewhere else — and a bit no code reads is worse than dead
code, because it still reads like policy. Each is now the presence or absence of a Grant
in the agent's own AgentDefinition: the adapter capability is a routed grant, and
raw_reads is simply whether the gather_raw path shape is in that agent's grant list.
The reader-lane program set is likewise not a JSON list: there is ONE shape per program
(runtime/permission/grant.program_shape), compiled from the GNU flag-arity facts.
